title: Using Hermes to automate social media posts
summary: Keith AI demonstrates using Hermes to automate and systematize social media posts. The video focuses on building repeatable processes that improve post quality rather than relying on guesswork.
writeup: The video demonstrates a programmatic system that retrieves all of a creator's posts and surfaces per-post engagement metrics (like count, reply count, and repost count) in one view. The central value is replacing intuition-based content decisions with data-driven feedback: seeing which posts actually performed lets a creator spot patterns and iterate deliberately rather than guessing. The through-line is that systematic measurement is the precondition for compounding improvement.

key_points: The system automatically discovers and aggregates all posts from an account alongside three engagement signals per post: likes, replies, and reposts.

Having engagement data collected in a single structured view makes it possible to identify which content types and topics are resonating versus which are underperforming, without relying on memory or gut feel.
The explicit goal is a compounding loop: each new post is informed by prior data, so performance should trend upward over time rather than staying flat or varying randomly.
